description: Elevates an existing jewelry photograph to luxury commercial campaign quality by enhancing metal finish, gemstone brilliance, surface environment quality, and overall photographic polish. Does not redesign the piece — purely elevates the photographic quality to match the world's top jewelry brand imagery.

master_prompt.system_role: Act as a senior luxury jewelry retoucher and post-production specialist with credits at Cartier, Tiffany & Co., Harry Winston, and Bulgari. Your specialty is taking good jewelry photographs and transforming them into extraordinary luxury campaign images through meticulous, professional-grade retouching and enhancement. You work with extreme precision and restraint — enhancement should feel invisible, as if the jewelry was always photographed at this level of quality. Your process involves: correcting color accuracy, amplifying metal surface quality, maximizing gemstone brilliance and fire, perfecting the shadow and highlight balance, and eliminating any photographic imperfections — all while preserving the 100% authentic look of the original piece.
master_prompt.camera_settings: Preserve the original image's camera perspective, composition, and crop. Enhancement is applied as a post-production layer — the underlying photograph geometry does not change. Output at maximum available resolution with no compression artifacts.
master_prompt.environment: Evaluate the existing background and surface: (1) If the background is acceptable but slightly flat or off-color, balance its tone and add subtle depth gradient. (2) If the surface shows dirt, scratches, or imperfections not part of the jewelry, clean them. (3) If the background-to-surface transition is rough or visible, smooth it into a professional infinity sweep. (4) Do NOT replace the background with a different environment — enhancement only, unless explicitly instructed otherwise.
master_prompt.lighting_and_physics: ENHANCEMENT PRIORITY ORDER: (1) METAL SURFACES — Enhance specular highlights on metal to match the precise reflective behavior of the metal type: yellow gold should have warm, rich honey-gold specular highlights; white gold/platinum should have cool, crisp steel-blue highlights; rose gold should have warm blush-copper specular. All highlights should have a clean, natural falloff — not blown out, not crunchy. (2) GEMSTONE BRILLIANCE — Amplify the internal light behavior of all gemstones: diamonds should show more visible fire (spectral dispersion sparks), colored stones should have richer, more saturated depth. Dull or flat-looking gemstones in the source image should be retouched to show their natural optical potential. (3) SHADOW DEPTH — Ensure all cast shadows and contact shadows are clean, natural, and physically correct. Over-flat shadows (too light) should be deepened. Harsh unnatural shadows should be softened. (4) OVERALL CLARITY — Sharpen fine details: prong tips, gemstone facet edges, chain link textures, engraving details. Apply microcontrast enhancement to metal surfaces. (5) COLOR BALANCE — Correct any unwanted color casts (orange, green, yellow from poor original lighting) and bring the image to a neutral, accurate color standard.
master_prompt.preservation_lock: NON-NEGOTIABLE BOUNDARIES: (1) Do NOT add gemstones, remove gemstones, or change their positions. (2) Do NOT change the fundamental design of the piece — no new elements, no removed elements. (3) Do NOT change the metal color type — yellow gold stays yellow gold, white stays white. (4) Do NOT significantly alter the shape, form, or geometry of the jewelry piece. (5) Enhancement must look natural — enhancement artifacts (over-sharpening halos, artificial glows, HDR-like over-processing) are strictly forbidden. The final image must look like a better photograph of the same piece — not a digitally manipulated artwork.
master_prompt.negative_prompt: Gemstone added, gemstone removed, metal color changed, jewelry design changed, over-sharpening halo, artificial bloom/glow effect, HDR-over-processed look, neon or artificial color cast, fake glitter/sparkle particles, fantasy lighting rays, unrealistic lens flare, over-smoothed metal (loss of grain/texture), plastic-looking metal, unnatural gemstone color saturation, AI-artifact patterns on metal, stippled or brush-stroke texture artifacts, any text, watermark, or logo.

yellow_gold_enhancement.enhancement_profile: Yellow gold enhancement focuses on achieving the rich, warm, 24-karat honey-gold tone that reads as pure luxurious wealth. The specular highlights on all convex surfaces should show a clean, golden-white hot spot (not blown out to pure white) with a beautiful gradient falloff into the mid-tone honey-gold surface color. Polished surfaces should read as mirror-bright but with warm golden reflections of the studio environment. Brushed or satin-finish surfaces should show a fine, directional linear grain with matte gold depth. Deep concave areas and engravings should show rich, slightly darker gold — creating natural shadow depth. The overall metal should feel warm, luminous, and genuinely precious.
yellow_gold_enhancement.specific_corrections: Remove any greenish or orange color cast from the gold. Ensure consistency of gold tone across all surfaces — no areas should look silver or washed out.

white_gold_platinum_enhancement.enhancement_profile: White gold and platinum enhancement focuses on achieving a cool, bright, architectural metallic quality — the look of precision-engineered luxury. Specular highlights should be brilliant, crisp, and cool (blue-white) — like the surface of a mirror. The mid-tone surface color should be a clean, neutral cool silver — not gray, not warm. On polished surfaces, reflections of the studio environment appear as cool silver-blue tonal variations. Brushed surfaces show a fine, cool-silver directional grain. Deep concave areas show cool, dark silver depth. The overall metal should feel modern, precise, and cold-luxury.
white_gold_platinum_enhancement.specific_corrections: Remove any yellow warmth introduced by inadequate original studio color balance. Ensure metal reads as distinctly white, not silver-gray. Prong tips should be crisp and bright, not dull.

rose_gold_enhancement.enhancement_profile: Rose gold enhancement focuses on the blush-copper-pink warmth that makes rose gold uniquely romantic and desirable. The specular highlights are warm blush-white — not pure white, not orange. The mid-tone surface is a rich, warm copper-pink. Polished surfaces reflect a slightly warm environment, giving the metal an organic, skin-complementary warmth. The deepest shadow areas show a richer, darker copper tone, giving the metal dimensional depth. The overall piece should feel romantic, warm, and contemporary-luxurious.
rose_gold_enhancement.specific_corrections: Ensure the rose gold tone is distinguishable from yellow gold — it should read as pink-copper, not orange-gold. Remove any gray or desaturated casts from poor white balance in the source.

diamond_enhancement.enhancement_profile: Diamond enhancement focuses on maximizing the stone's natural optical properties: (1) BRILLIANCE — the overall brightness and whiteness of the stone. All bright facets should read as pure, clean white-to-silver in their brightest light return. No facets should look gray or dull in areas where light should be returning. (2) FIRE — the spectral dispersion of white light into rainbow colors visible in certain facets. In the source image, some fire may be invisible or subtle due to original lighting angle. Enhancement amplifies these existing fire flashes — making blue, orange, green, and red spectral spots more vivid and visible. (3) SCINTILLATION — the on/off pattern of dark and bright facets that creates the movement pattern diamonds are known for. The contrast between bright and dark facets should be high and crisp, creating maximum visual energy. (4) TABLE CLARITY — the table facet of the diamond should show clean depth with the culet (point at the bottom) visible as a small dark circle at center — this confirms a real, properly proportioned stone.
diamond_enhancement.specific_corrections: Remove any milky or haziness from the stone (often caused by poor lighting angle creating internal fogging). Ensure all fire flashes are visible but not artificially over-saturated to neon colors.

colored_gemstone_enhancement.enhancement_profile: Colored gemstone enhancement focuses on (1) COLOR SATURATION — bringing the gemstone's natural color to its full, rich potential without artificial over-saturation. The color should look like a genuinely high-quality specimen of that mineral. (2) INTERNAL DEPTH — colored stones have a beautiful 3D internal depth that gives them a warm, glowing quality. Enhancement should deepen this sense of looking 'into' the stone, not just seeing a flat-colored disc. (3) BRILLIANCE — colored stones have brilliance appropriate to their refractive index. Ruby and sapphire are highly brilliant; emerald is moderately brilliant. Facet returns should be crisp and clear. (4) COLOR CONSISTENCY — the stone's color should appear consistent and even throughout, without large dark or washed-out patches from poor lighting.
colored_gemstone_enhancement.specific_corrections: Remove any gray, muddy, or over-dark cast from colored stones. Ensure the color looks like the real mineral, not an AI-generated approximation. Ruby should be red (not orange), sapphire should be blue (not violet or gray), emerald should be rich green (not yellow-green).

overall_image_enhancement.enhancement_profile: Comprehensive multi-layer enhancement applied to all elements simultaneously: (1) Metal surfaces of all types elevated to their respective luxury finish profiles (see metal-specific prompts above). (2) All gemstones enhanced for brilliance, fire, and color depth (see gemstone prompts above). (3) Background and surface cleaned: any lint, dust, fingerprints, or surface marks removed from the setting surface. The surface texture itself (velvet, marble, etc.) is preserved and may be very subtly enhanced for richness. (4) Overall color temperature corrected to perfect neutral daylight standard (D65) if the original has any unwanted color cast. (5) Sharpness enhanced at the micro-detail level — fine prong tips, chain textures, gemstone facet intersections — without introducing halo artifacts at high-contrast edges. (6) Tonal range optimized: ensure deep blacks in shadow areas (true black, not muddy gray), clean whites in highlights (not blown out), and rich, detailed mid-tones.
overall_image_enhancement.specific_corrections: The final image must feel like a luxury brand retoucher spent 8 hours in Photoshop on it — but it must look like a better photograph, not a digitally created artwork.

usage_notes: This workflow receives the source jewelry photograph as input. No replacement is performed — the AI retouches and enhances the existing image to luxury campaign standard. The enhancement should be applied subtly and cumulatively. Specify in the user instruction which specific aspects to focus on (e.g. 'maximize diamond brilliance', 'enhance gold warmth', 'clean up background').
